name: New America Corporation
description_short: Non-profit organization providing housing, education, and business development services to build equitable communities through scholarships, rental programs, and capital access.
description_long:
New America Corporation is a South Carolina-based non-profit organization founded with a mission to build strong, vibrant, diverse, and equitable communities. The organization focuses on three core pillars: housing services, education, and business development. According to SCACED (South Carolina Association for Community Economic Development), they are listed as a member organization specializing in housing services and education programming.

The organization operates four key programs: the New America Leadership Scholarship (education-focused), New America Capital (business financing), New America Rentals (affordable housing access), and New America Development & Construction (real estate development). Their comprehensive approach suggests they serve as a community development organization addressing multiple aspects of economic mobility and community stability simultaneously.

What distinguishes New America Corporation is their integrated model combining education, housing, and business capital in a single organization. Rather than specializing in one vertical, they address interconnected barriers to economic opportunity. Their focus on construction and development suggests direct real estate involvement beyond just rental management, indicating deeper community infrastructure work.

Limitations include minimal public information available beyond their SCACED directory listing. The website URL provided (www.new-america.org) and phone number (803-534-0035) were sourced from the directory but the organization's full service scope, funding sources, specific scholarship amounts, rental program details, and capital loan terms cannot be verified from available documentation. Prospective users should contact them directly to understand program eligibility and current offerings.
pros: Operates multiple integrated programs addressing housing, education, and business capital in one organization
Offers New America Leadership Scholarship providing education access
Provides New America Capital program enabling business financing for entrepreneurs
Manages New America Rentals providing affordable housing access
Engages in New America Development & Construction suggesting direct community real estate investment
Listed as SCACED member indicating recognition by South Carolina economic development community
Mission explicitly prioritizes equity and diversity in community building
cons: Extremely limited public information available beyond directory listing; full program details not verified
No documented certification or accreditation details provided on directory page
Geographic scope unclear—appears South Carolina-based but service area not specified
Program eligibility requirements, income limits, and specific terms not documented
No details on scholarship amounts, loan caps, rental requirements, or approval processes
best_for: South Carolina residents seeking affordable housing with supportive services
Entrepreneurs and small business owners needing access to capital in underserved communities
Students pursuing education opportunities through merit-based scholarships
diagnosis: New America Corporation is best for South Carolina residents seeking integrated support across housing, education, and business development from a community-focused non-profit. The primary caveat is that detailed program information, eligibility criteria, and current service availability are not publicly documented—direct contact via phone (803-534-0035) or their website is necessary to determine specific program fit and access.
services: New America Capital (business financing and capital access)
New America Rentals (affordable rental housing program)
New America Development & Construction (real estate development)
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
